transcript: The sight of 50,000 sky blue and white flags wave. Manchester City against Tottenham Hotspur part two. Spurs 1-0 lead means this tie is firmly in the balance. And it could be a Champions League classic. De Bruyne and he finds Sterling Sterling's had a go everything he touches at the moment is golden. What a start for City. Another glorious goal from Raheem Sterling. Lucas Moura Dele Alli Chance to shoot for Son Spurs are level Son has beaten Ederson with a brilliant finish. Are we seven minutes in or are we 70 minutes in? What a start to this game. Lucas Moura, finding Eriksen, finding Son, Son's shot, 2-1 Tottenham, 2-1 Tottenham This has to be the craziest nine and a half minutes that I've witnessed in football Aguero, Bernardo Silva is over, here's Bernardo Silva, it's deflected in, it's Sterling again Ronaldo Silva to Kevin De Bruyne and it's starting again all the way across goal from Kevin De Bruyne what a combination these two are De Bruyne, who's pulled it back for Aguero. Vital block by Alderweireld. Ericsson over to Son, all the over Walker can't stop him shooting goodness that was close It's De Bruyne, and it whistles just too high. De Bruyne, Gundogan, David Silva, Sterling, Lloris shoveled it out. It's wonderful attacking play, but it's even better defensive play from Danny Rose. De Bruyne's shot brilliantly kept out by Lloris. It's a sparkling save. Edurente, straight at Ederson, cleared by Laporte and all of a sudden... Gundogan, De Bruyne effortlessly takes the ball into his stride and goes past Tudor to find Aguero Aguero! Trippier Thank you for watching. Trip here. Son Heung-min, really good run. Really good goalkeeping. Llorente is tussling with Mendy. He's got away from him here though. Company! It's come off Llorente! It's gone in! The advantage swings back to Spurs. There's Aguero And it's held by Lloris Sané wants it at the back Sanéé over the top from Gundogan, Eriksen, that's going to come to Sergio Aguero, Aguero to Sterling, Sterling! Raheem Sterling wins the game all over again My goodness, as if there couldn't be any more drama Oh, that's remarkable The goal's chalked out And Spurs can celebrate all over again VAR has come to the Champions League It's Tottenham Thank you. Spurs can celebrate all over again. VAR has come to the Champions League. It's Tottenham. And for all Spurs around the world, it's terrific. Place in the final four of the Champions League. A Champions League night that hasn't had many to match it for drama. And with the new introduction of VAR, there's a whole new level of drama.
